title: Users, Roles & Tablespaces Management
description: Create and manage Oracle database users, roles, and tablespaces with proper privilege management and space allocation.
objectives: Create and manage Oracle database users
Grant system and object privileges to users

tasks.title: Create Three Database Users
tasks.description: Create three new users with restricted privileges. These users will represent different application roles with varying access levels.
tasks.code:
-- Connect as SYSDBA
CONNECT / AS SYSDBA

-- Create User 1: APP_USER01
CREATE USER app_user01 IDENTIFIED BY Welcome123
  DEFAULT TABLESPACE users
  QUOTA 100M ON users;

-- Create User 2: APP_USER02
CREATE USER app_user02 IDENTIFIED BY Welcome123
  DEFAULT TABLESPACE users
  QUOTA 100M ON users;

-- Create User 3: APP_USER03
CREATE USER app_user03 IDENTIFIED BY Welcome123
  DEFAULT TABLESPACE users
  QUOTA 100M ON users;
tasks.expectedOutput:
User APP_USER01 created.
User APP_USER02 created.
User APP_USER03 created.
tasks.note: The QUOTA clause limits the amount of space each user can consume in the USERS tablespace. This prevents accidental space exhaustion by individual users.

tasks.title: Grant CONNECT Privilege to All Users
tasks.description: Grant the CONNECT privilege (which includes CREATE SESSION) to enable users to connect to the database. Also grant basic CREATE privileges for app development.
tasks.note: CONNECT is a role that includes CREATE SESSION, RESOURCE, and other basic privileges. Alternatively, you can grant CREATE SESSION directly for more granular control.

tasks.title: Create a Composite Role for DML Operations
tasks.description: Create a custom role that bundles all the permissions needed for application users to perform INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations on existing tables.
tasks.note: UNLIMITED TABLESPACE grants the role user the ability to use unlimited space in all tablespaces. Use with caution in production; consider specific quotas instead.

tasks.title: Assign the Role to the Third User
tasks.description: Assign the app_dml_role to app_user03, giving this user all the bundled permissions at once. This demonstrates the power of roles for privilege management.
tasks.expectedOutput:
Grant succeeded.

GRANTEE      GRANTED_ROLE          ADMIN_OPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
APP_USER03   APP_DML_ROLE          NO
tasks.note: The ADMIN_OPTION set to 'NO' means app_user03 cannot grant this role to other users. Set ADMIN_OPTION to YES if this is required.

tasks.title: Create Tablespace for User 1
tasks.description: Create a dedicated tablespace for app_user01. This provides storage isolation and makes it easier to manage user-specific data and performance.
tasks.code:
-- Create tablespace for app_user01
CREATE TABLESPACE app_user01_ts
  DATAFILE '/oracle/oradata/app_user01_01.dbf'
  SIZE 100M
  AUTOEXTEND ON
  NEXT 10M
  MAXSIZE 500M
  EXTENT MANAGEMENT LOCAL
  SEGMENT SPACE MANAGEMENT AUTO;

-- Assign as default tablespace for user1
ALTER USER app_user01 DEFAULT TABLESPACE app_user01_ts;
ALTER USER app_user01 QUOTA 200M ON app_user01_ts;
tasks.expectedOutput:
Tablespace created.
User altered.
User altered.
tasks.note: AUTOEXTEND allows the tablespace to grow automatically. EXTENT MANAGEMENT LOCAL uses bitmaps for faster extent allocation.

tasks.title: Create Tablespace for User 2
tasks.description: Create a separate tablespace for app_user02 with the same configuration pattern as User 1.
tasks.code:
-- Create tablespace for app_user02
CREATE TABLESPACE app_user02_ts
  DATAFILE '/oracle/oradata/app_user02_01.dbf'
  SIZE 100M
  AUTOEXTEND ON
  NEXT 10M
  MAXSIZE 500M
  EXTENT MANAGEMENT LOCAL
  SEGMENT SPACE MANAGEMENT AUTO;

-- Assign as default tablespace for user2
ALTER USER app_user02 DEFAULT TABLESPACE app_user02_ts;
ALTER USER app_user02 QUOTA 200M ON app_user02_ts;
tasks.expectedOutput:
Tablespace created.
User altered.
User altered.

tasks.title: Create Tablespace for User 3
tasks.description: Create the final tablespace for app_user03. This user will benefit from the DML role's expanded privileges within their own tablespace.
tasks.code:
-- Create tablespace for app_user03
CREATE TABLESPACE app_user03_ts
  DATAFILE '/oracle/oradata/app_user03_01.dbf'
  SIZE 100M
  AUTOEXTEND ON
  NEXT 10M
  MAXSIZE 500M
  EXTENT MANAGEMENT LOCAL
  SEGMENT SPACE MANAGEMENT AUTO;

-- Assign as default tablespace for user3
ALTER USER app_user03 DEFAULT TABLESPACE app_user03_ts;
ALTER USER app_user03 QUOTA 200M ON app_user03_ts;
tasks.expectedOutput:
Tablespace created.
User altered.
User altered.

tasks.title: Add Secondary Datafiles to Tablespaces
tasks.description: Add additional datafiles to each tablespace to increase storage capacity and distribute I/O across multiple disk spindles for performance optimization.
tasks.code:
-- Add secondary datafile to User 1 tablespace
ALTER TABLESPACE app_user01_ts
  ADD DATAFILE '/oracle/oradata/app_user01_02.dbf'
  SIZE 100M
  AUTOEXTEND ON
  NEXT 10M
  MAXSIZE 500M;

-- Add secondary datafile to User 2 tablespace
ALTER TABLESPACE app_user02_ts
  ADD DATAFILE '/oracle/oradata/app_user02_02.dbf'
  SIZE 100M
  AUTOEXTEND ON
  NEXT 10M
  MAXSIZE 500M;

-- Add secondary datafile to User 3 tablespace
ALTER TABLESPACE app_user03_ts
  ADD DATAFILE '/oracle/oradata/app_user03_02.dbf'
  SIZE 100M
  AUTOEXTEND ON
  NEXT 10M
  MAXSIZE 500M;
tasks.note: Multiple datafiles allow Oracle to stripe I/O operations across different storage devices, improving query performance.

tasks.title: Set Space Usage Thresholds to 85%
tasks.description: Configure space alerts on all tablespaces to trigger warnings when they reach 85% capacity. This provides proactive monitoring to prevent running out of space.
tasks.expectedOutput:
Tablespace altered.
PL/SQL procedure successfully completed. (repeated 3 times)
tasks.warning: Alerts are triggered when threshold is reached. Monitor the alert log and respond promptly to prevent tablespace full errors.

verification.title: Verification: Check Your Work
verification.description: Run these queries to verify that all users, roles, tablespaces, and thresholds have been configured correctly.
verification.code:
-- Verify users were created
SELECT username, default_tablespace, created
FROM dba_users
WHERE username IN ('APP_USER01', 'APP_USER02', 'APP_USER03')
ORDER BY username;

-- Verify role was created and assigned
SELECT grantee, granted_role
FROM dba_role_privs
WHERE grantee IN ('APP_USER01', 'APP_USER02', 'APP_USER03', 'APP_DML_ROLE');

-- Verify tablespaces were created
SELECT tablespace_name, status, extent_management, segment_space_management
FROM dba_tablespaces
WHERE tablespace_name IN ('APP_USER01_TS', 'APP_USER02_TS', 'APP_USER03_TS')
ORDER BY tablespace_name;

-- Verify datafiles
SELECT tablespace_name, file_name, bytes/1024/1024 AS size_mb, autoextensible
FROM dba_data_files
WHERE tablespace_name IN ('APP_USER01_TS', 'APP_USER02_TS', 'APP_USER03_TS')
ORDER BY tablespace_name, file_name;

-- Check user quotas
SELECT username, tablespace_name, max_bytes/1024/1024 AS quota_mb
FROM dba_ts_quotas
WHERE username IN ('APP_USER01', 'APP_USER02', 'APP_USER03')
ORDER BY username;
checklist: All three users (APP_USER01, APP_USER02, APP_USER03) created
CONNECT privilege granted to all users
Each user has 200M quota in their default tablespace
Space thresholds set to 85% on all tablespaces

exercises.title: Create another role
exercises.description: Create another role called 'APP_READONLY_ROLE' that grants only SELECT privileges, then assign it to APP_USER01

exercises.title: Add a third datafile
exercises.description: Add a third datafile to app_user02_ts and increase the tablespace size to 400M

exercises.title: Query the threshold settings
exercises.description: Query the threshold settings using DBA_TABLESPACE_THRESHOLD_PARAMETERS to verify the 85% threshold

exercises.title: Create a test table
exercises.description: Create a test table in each user's tablespace and insert sample data to verify their access

exercises.title: Generate a space usage report
exercises.description: Generate a space usage report showing the current percentage of space used in each tablespace
